details.body:
For consistency can we change this to a lowercase `o` in order to ensure compatibility with pre-existing SDKs that customers may be using? i.e. `onCertificateConflictAction`.

OR, is this something that happens automatically when we use TypeSpec conversion. i.e. That it converts all types to capital? If that's the case, is it possible that some other types for exist resources may have been capitalized, and might this potentially cause problems for existing customers SDKs?

details.body: Talking to Dylan. In the existing SDK that's being used by QA, the type here is `akvDetails`. Can we keep akvDetails please? Also, why did this happen? Are there similar changes anywhere else in the TypeSpec that might have crept in that will now different type names? This would affect customer automation/SDK use.

events.description: One thing unrelated to the PR here is that when I render the Swagger, the Status field is shown in t

details.body:
One thing unrelated to the PR here is that when I render the Swagger, the Status field is shown in the PUT Bucket request body. Even though it's marked as a read-only property. Probably nothing to worry about, and may just be related to the Swagger Viewer.

When I compare what's here in the TypeSpec file with what was in the original Swagger, I see the read-only attribute is applied at the point of reference, and not directly inside the enum definition.

```
        "status": {
          "

events.description: I've had a look at the TypeSpec generated Swagger and it looks as though this capitalization has occ

details.body: I've had a look at the TypeSpec generated Swagger and it looks as though this capitalization has occurred pretty much across the board. If we sure this wont cause issues, then there's no need to change this `onCertificateConflictAction` property.

events.description: Not a worry, read only fields can be expected in PUT requests they are just ignored by RP.

details.body:
Not a worry, read only fields can be expected in PUT requests they are just ignored by RP. 
Common scenario is clients GET the resource, edit the properties they want in the response payload, then PUT the entire resource, including read only properties. So RP should just ignore them or optionaly they can return an error if the readOnly value has changed.

events.description: More verbose naming is encouraged since that adds clarity. This is for public consumption and will r

details.body:
More verbose naming is encouraged since that adds clarity. This is for public consumption and will reflect in public reference documentation and offical Azure SDK code.   
There are no customers using this type it is new in this version so we would not be breaking. 

We could change yes that is very doable. But first the question is should we? Well the answer is not nessesarly. At least we should not based on internal QA SDKs name alone. We should not care about that here. What matters is the
Types should be capitalized.

events.description: Okay. Will leave it as is so. i.e. `AzureKeyVaultDetails`. It's not out in the open, and only QA is

details.body: Okay. Will leave it as is so. i.e. `AzureKeyVaultDetails`. It's not out in the open, and only QA is affected. But if we're going to be re-generating the QA SDK from the TypeSpec generated Swagge anyway, then it'll pick up the new name, so there shouldn't be issues.
```
